raw_body:
Microsoft has announced the wider testing of its new Autopilot feature at the Microsoft Build event this week, backed by a post on the company’s’ website. Autopilots are described as a new category of agents that can work autonomously on a user’s behalf. Microsoft says each Autopilot has its own identity, and so multiple agents […]
The post Scout from M’Soft is the agentic Autopilot that works across M365 appeared first on AI News.
speedrun: Microsoft has introduced a new feature called Autopilot, designed to work autonomously for users across its M365 platform. Each Autopilot has a unique identity, allowing multiple agents to operate simultaneously. This development, showcased at the Microsoft Build event, represents a significant step in enhancing user productivity and efficiency. As more users test this feature, it could reshape how people interact with technology in their daily tasks.
why_it_matters: This could streamline workflows for professionals who rely on M365, allowing them to focus on more complex tasks.
The introduction of agentic Autopilots could signify a shift toward more personalized and efficient AI solutions in the workplace.

lenses.eli12: Microsoft's new Autopilot feature acts like a personal assistant that can handle tasks on your behalf. Each Autopilot has its own personality, meaning they can work together without confusion. This development could make everyday tasks easier and quicker for users, allowing them to spend time on what really matters.
lenses.pm: For product managers, the Autopilot feature addresses a strong user need for efficiency by automating routine tasks in M365. This could reduce operational costs and improve user satisfaction. As users begin to adopt these agents, it’s essential to gather feedback to refine their capabilities.
lenses.engineer: The Autopilot feature leverages advanced AI to create unique identities for each agent, allowing them to operate autonomously within M365. This could involve using state-of-the-art models for natural language processing and task management. However, details on specific benchmarks or performance metrics have not been disclosed yet.
